Culture Calendar — Full content dump for LLMs
============================================================
URL: https://hadrien-cornier.github.io/Culture-Calendar/
Generated: 2026-07-22T18:49:53Z

Austin cultural events, AI-curated. Films, concerts, opera, ballet, book clubs, and visual arts — sorted by merit, not marketing.

About
-----
# How this works

The Austin Culture Tipsheet is an opinionated weekly guide to films, concerts, opera, ballet, and literary events around Austin. It is assembled by an automated pipeline and then sorted by merit — not by chronology or venue marketing budget.

## Venues

Each week the pipeline scrapes or loads schedules from:

- Austin Film Society
- Hyperreal Film Club
- Paramount Theatre
- Austin Symphony
- Early Music Austin
- La Follia Austin Baroque
- Austin Opera
- Ballet Austin
- Alienated Majesty Books
- First Light Austin
- Arts on Alexander

Venues are added by hand. If a venue is missing, it is because no one has written a scraper for it yet.

## Ratings

Every event carries a single score on a 0–10 scale. Ratings are generated by a language model under the direction of a critic-style prompt and are intended to reflect three things in roughly this order:

1. **Artistic merit** — craft, performance quality, directorial voice, compositional depth.
2. **Originality** — how unusual the work is relative to what is typically on offer in Austin this month.
3. **Cultural significance** — durability, canonical weight, relevance to live conversations in the arts.

A rating of 7 or above is the threshold for "worth your evening"; 8+ enters the weekly Top 10. Ratings are stable across reruns for the same event unless upstream data changes.

## Reviews

Reviews are written by Anthropic's Claude and Perplexity's Sonar working in sequence: Claude drafts the critical voice and the one-line hook, Sonar supplies factual scaffolding (cast, director, release year, composer dates, premiere context) sourced from Wikipedia, Letterboxd, and publisher metadata for the pilot event set. The tone aims for a working critic's voice — declarative, specific, unafraid of a verdict — rather than press-release prose.

## Mailing list

A weekly digest lands in your inbox every Monday morning with the Top 10 picks for the week ahead. One email per week — no daily blasts, no transactional noise, no cross-promotion. The list is operated on Buttondown's free tier; we store only the email address you provide.

Every issue carries a one-click unsubscribe link in the footer, and you can opt out at any time by visiting the unsubscribe page linked from `/archive.html`. Unsubscribing is immediate and removes your address from the list without follow-up email. The signup form on the masthead is the only place we collect addresses, and we do not share or sell them.

## Limitations

A few things to keep in mind:

- **Refusal edge cases.** Occasionally the model refuses to review an event (too little metadata, a name collision, a safety filter trip). The pipeline now filters those out at the description level, but earlier snapshots of the site may still show them.
- **Review churn.** Re-running the pipeline can produce a subtly different review for the same event. The rating is much more stable than the prose.
- **No human editorial step.** Nothing here has been fact-checked by a human before publication. Treat factual claims (dates, credits, lineages) as a starting point, not a citation.

If something looks wrong, it probably is. Corrections welcome.

Statistics
----------
Total events: 243
- Book club: 26
- Concert: 44
- Dance: 28
- Film: 118
- Opera: 12
- Other: 12
- Visual arts: 3

Venues:
- AFS: 39
- AlienatedMajesty: 13
- ArtsOnAlexander: 13
- BalletAustin: 28
- Central Library Gallery: 1
- Chamber Music: 9
- EarlyMusic: 8
- FirstLight: 3
- Hyperreal: 21
- LivraBooks: 20
- NewYorkerMeetup: 2
- Opera: 12
- Paramount: 58
- Symphony: 14
- Unchained.Art Contemporary Gallery: 1
- Women & Their Work: 1

Top events (ranked by rating, up to 30)
---------------------------------------

1. [10/10] GOODFELLAS
AFS | 2026-07-23 | 19:15
Hook: Scorsese's mob comedy roars through betrayal, paranoia, and perfect pop irony.
Review: ★ Rating: 10/10

🎭 Artistic Merit – Scorsese turns the rise and rot of a mobster into a machine of constant motion. The Copacabana tracking shot, Henry Hill’s jittery voiceover, the freeze frames, the propulsive edits, and the use of pop songs as irony all work together with ruthless control. Joe Pesci’s Tommy, especially in the “Funny how?” scene, brings sudden volatility that makes every room feel unsafe. The later stretch, when Henry is coked-up, paranoid, and lugging guns, women, and groceries through the day, has the tight panic of a pressure cooker.

✨ Originality – The film avoids the fake nobility usually attached to gangster stories. Instead of glamour with a warning label, it gives you glamour, then shows the petty logistics, boredom, and cowardice underneath it. The voiceover i…
URL: https://hadrien-cornier.github.io/Culture-Calendar/events/goodfellas.html

2. [10/10] SPIRITED AWAY
AFS | 2026-07-24 | 16:15
Hook: Miyazaki’s hand-drawn bathhouse world moves with the weight of a mop dragged across a floor.
Review: ★ Rating: 10/10

🎭 Artistic Merit 
Hayao Miyazaki, in this 2001 feature, builds a world with unusual control. The opening road scene, the abandoned theme park, and Chihiro’s first look at the bathhouse all establish place through motion, texture, and sound rather than exposition. The hand-drawn animation gives weight to small acts, a mop dragged across a floor, soot spirits hauling coal, a train sliding across black water. The result is precise and alive, with a visual discipline that keeps the film moving even when it turns strange.

✨ Originality 
The film refuses familiar fantasy logic. It mixes child anxiety, labor, greed, environmental damage, and folklore without flattening them into a lesson plan. The stink spirit sequence turns disgust into comic cleanup, then into an image of pol…
URL: https://hadrien-cornier.github.io/Culture-Calendar/events/spirited-away.html

3. [10/10] Goodfellas
Paramount | 2026-08-02 | 19:00
Hook: Scorsese's mob comedy roars through betrayal, paranoia, and perfect pop irony.
Review: ★ Rating: 10/10

🎭 Artistic Merit – Scorsese turns the rise and rot of a mobster into a machine of constant motion. The Copacabana tracking shot, Henry Hill’s jittery voiceover, the freeze frames, the propulsive edits, and the use of pop songs as irony all work together with ruthless control. Joe Pesci’s Tommy, especially in the “Funny how?” scene, brings sudden volatility that makes every room feel unsafe. The later stretch, when Henry is coked-up, paranoid, and lugging guns, women, and groceries through the day, has the tight panic of a pressure cooker.

✨ Originality – The film avoids the fake nobility usually attached to gangster stories. Instead of glamour with a warning label, it gives you glamour, then shows the petty logistics, boredom, and cowardice underneath it. The voiceover i…

4. [10/10] Spirited Away
Paramount | 2026-08-02 | 15:00
Hook: Miyazaki’s hand-drawn bathhouse world moves with the weight of a mop dragged across a floor.
Review: ★ Rating: 10/10

🎭 Artistic Merit 
Hayao Miyazaki, in this 2001 feature, builds a world with unusual control. The opening road scene, the abandoned theme park, and Chihiro’s first look at the bathhouse all establish place through motion, texture, and sound rather than exposition. The hand-drawn animation gives weight to small acts, a mop dragged across a floor, soot spirits hauling coal, a train sliding across black water. The result is precise and alive, with a visual discipline that keeps the film moving even when it turns strange.

✨ Originality 
The film refuses familiar fantasy logic. It mixes child anxiety, labor, greed, environmental damage, and folklore without flattening them into a lesson plan. The stink spirit sequence turns disgust into comic cleanup, then into an image of pol…

5. [10/10] Citizen Kane
Paramount | 2026-08-30 | 19:00
Hook: A dying tycoon's snow globe shatters into a century of cinematic ambition.
Review: ★ Rating: 10/10

🎭 Artistic Merit , Citizen Kane is a technical shock from the opening frames, with the dying whisper, the snow globe, and the barbed wire silhouette of Xanadu setting a standard for visual storytelling that still looks unusually exacting. Gregg Toland’s deep-focus photography, the angular lighting, and the aggressive sound design give every room pressure and dimension, while Welles’s editing keeps the film moving with constant formal invention rather than decorative flourish.

✨ Originality , Its structure is still the key surprise, a fractured obituary built from competing testimonies, not a straight rise-and-fall biography. The film refuses the easy answer about Kane, and that refusal is built into scenes like the breakfast-table montage, where a marriage collapses in a…
URL: https://hadrien-cornier.github.io/Culture-Calendar/events/citizen-kane.html

6. [9/10] AKIRA
Hyperreal | 2026-07-02 | 19:30
Hook: Hand-drawn frames surge through neon rain into blinding abstract geometry.
Review: ★ Rating: 9/10

🎭 Artistic Merit 
Akira delivers phenomenal hand-drawn animation that set the visual standard for modern anime, with every frame dense in neon color, motion blur, and architectural detail . The film’s kinetic energy peaks in the final thirty minutes, where Tetsuo’s cellular mutation unfolds as a harrowing, surreal cascade of body horror and cosmic dissolution . Sound design and Isaac Asimov-esque musical tension amplify the chaos, particularly during Kaneda’s motorcycle chase through Neo-Tokyo’s collapsing streets, where the bike’s engine screams against the city’s structural failure . The direction by Katsuhiro Otomo, who also authored the source manga, remains uncompromising in pacing and visual fidelity, resulting in a technically masterful, if occasionally overwhelming…
URL: https://hadrien-cornier.github.io/Culture-Calendar/events/akira.html

7. [9/10] TAMPOPO
Hyperreal | 2026-07-08 | 19:30
Hook: A ramen Western with sports-movie training montages and perfect comic rhythm.
Review: ★ Rating: 9/10

🎭 Artistic Merit – Juzo Itami’s Tampopo executes a jerry-rigged but brilliant structure that marries the throughline of a Western with the training-montage logic of an American sports film . The film opens with truck driver Goro (Tsutomu Yamazaki) riding a semi rather than a horse, immediately establishing a visual joke that sustains the entire runtime . Itami layers meta-commentary delivered straight to the camera by Kōji Yakusho’s gangster-in-a-white-suit, creating a self-aware texture that feels both sui generis and like the Japanese equivalent of peak ZAZ comedy . The cinematography avoids the rich gloss of Hollywood; instead, it frames the ramen preparation with the same gritty focus as a fist fight or a dentistry scene, grounding the absurdity in physical reality .…
URL: https://hadrien-cornier.github.io/Culture-Calendar/events/tampopo.html

8. [9/10] HAUSU
Hyperreal | 2026-07-15 | 19:30
Hook: Psychedelic horror that devours reality with gleeful, kinetic chaos.
Review: ★ Rating: 9/10

🎭 Artistic Merit – Nobuhiko Obayashi’s Hausu (1977) executes a surreal horror-comedy with a distinct, original visual style that rejects conventional cinematography for psychedelic collage and Scooby-Doo-era cartoon effects. The film’s opening titles immediately break the fourth wall by introducing itself as “a movie,” signaling a self-aware, darkly comic tone that persists through every scene. Technical mastery shines in the way mundane objects—like a watermelon in a well or a white cat—suddenly become deadly agents, turning domestic familiarity into grotesque threat. The psychedelic soundtrack and retro animation effects create a fever-dream aesthetic that feels both 1970s-authentic and experimentally bold.

✨ Originality – Hausu avoids cookie-cutter horror patterns by f…
URL: https://hadrien-cornier.github.io/Culture-Calendar/events/hausu.html

9. [9/10] MILLENNIUM ACTRESS
AFS | 2026-07-25 | 19:00
Hook: Satoshi Kon collapses time and genre into a seamless memory chase.
Review: ★ Rating: 9/10

🎭 Artistic Merit
Satoshi Kon executes a narrative machine that fuses documentary form with surreal fantasy, using the demolition of a film studio as the physical anchor for Chiyoko Fujiwara’s memory. The film’s technical mastery lies in its seamless visual transitions where the elderly actress’s recollections dissolve into the sets of war dramas, samurai chanbara stories, and sci-fi epics, with the documentary crew Genya Tachibana and Kyoji Ida physically appearing within these historical vignettes . Kon avoids the static framing of a standard interview; instead, he animates the past with a kinetic energy that matches the genre shifts, from the Sino-Japanese War era to a distant future, ensuring the animation style evolves to match the story being told . The pacing is rele…
URL: https://hadrien-cornier.github.io/Culture-Calendar/events/millennium-actress.html

10. [9/10] SULLIVAN’S TRAVELS
AFS | 2026-07-25 | 14:00
Hook: Preston Sturges’s Depression-era lark turns brutal, then finds grace in a chain gang.
Review: ★ Rating: 9/10

🎭 Artistic Merit – Preston Sturges executes a screwball comedy with the precision of a courtroom drama, balancing rapid-fire dialogue and physical gags without losing narrative control. The opening sequence crackles with signature wit, establishing a rhythm that sustains through the chain gang climax, where the film’s tone shifts from farce to stark realism without breaking pace . Joel McCrea’s performance grounds the absurdity; his panic when beaten by the chain gang boss feels earned, not manufactured, and Veronica Lake’s cynical “The Girl” delivers a counterpoint that sharpens the satire of Hollywood pretension . The black-and-white cinematography, shot in 1.37 aspect ratio, uses shadow to underscore the chain gang’s brutality, a visual choice that elevates the film bey…
URL: https://hadrien-cornier.github.io/Culture-Calendar/events/sullivan’s-travels.html

11. [9/10] Strangers on a Train
Paramount | 2026-07-29 | 19:30
Hook: Hitchcock’s exacting visual logic tightens a thriller of swapped murders and polished menace.
Review: ★ Rating: 9/10

🎭 Artistic Merit, Hitchcock’s control is exacting. The train compartments, tennis court glare, carnival machinery, and shadowed interiors are all staged with a hard visual logic that keeps the film taut even when the plot turns grotesque. The performances do heavy lifting too, especially Robert Walker’s polished menace, which never tips into camp, and Farley Granger’s brittle panic, which gives the story its nervous pulse.

✨ Originality, The film’s central bargain, two strangers trading murders, is still a clean, nasty idea. What gives it force is how quickly it moves from gimmick to moral trap. The doubles structure, the crossed motives, and the public spaces turned into sites of private terror keep the film from settling into routine thriller logic. Bruno is not just a…
URL: https://hadrien-cornier.github.io/Culture-Calendar/events/strangers-on-a-train.html

12. [9/10] ALL THAT JAZZ
Hyperreal | 2026-07-31 | 19:30
Hook: Fosse's dazzling, brutal self-portrait: a pill-popping showman dances with death.
Review: ★ Rating: 9/10

🎭 Artistic Merit – Bob Fosse’s direction is a collision of show-business sleaze and metaphysical hallucination, executed with relentless precision. Roy Scheider’s performance as Joe Gideon is the film’s anchor: he embodies a pill-popping, chain-smoking workaholic who skips his daughter’s weekend to edit a film or catch a dancer in bed . The opening audition scene where Gideon chooses dancers for his show while simultaneously selecting a woman for the night establishes the film’s brutal rhythm of transactional intimacy . Fosse’s choreography—particularly the “Take Off With Us” orgy sequence—mixes tits, sweat, and cigarette smoke into an “exquisitely choreographed orgy” that feels both seductive and grotesque . The film’s final number suffers from slightly off sound mixing,…
URL: https://hadrien-cornier.github.io/Culture-Calendar/events/all-that-jazz.html

13. [9/10] ANDREI RUBLEV
AFS | 2026-07-31 | 20:20
Hook: Tarkovsky's three-hour medieval epic balances mud, faith, and divine light.
Review: ★ Rating: 9/10

🎭 Artistic Merit – Tarkovsky’s craft is total and unyielding, turning medieval Russia into a living, breathing world of mud, blood, and light. The film’s three-hour-and-25-minute scale is not a burden but a necessity; every frame serves the story’s elliptical, symbol-driven architecture. Specific sequences like the silver bell casting—where the unknown boy reveals his genius and the bell rings with a sound that “scourges the world temporarily of the evil” —show how Tarkovsky merges material and spiritual in a way few directors have achieved. The mise en scène is dense with detail: snake swimming through muddy pools, cotton motes in sunlight, endless flower fields . These are not decorative flourishes but evidence of God as Tarkovsky sees it. The camera moves between intima…
URL: https://hadrien-cornier.github.io/Culture-Calendar/events/andrei-rublev.html

14. [9/10] SOLARIS
AFS | 2026-07-31 | 17:00
Hook: Tarkovsky's space station becomes a theater of guilt, grief, and spectral return.
Review: ★ Rating: 9/10

🎭 Artistic Merit 
Tarkovsky executes a 166-minute ascent into inner space with the precision of a surgeon and the patience of a monk. The film’s opening sequence—Kelvin (Donatas Banionis) walking through his childhood dacha, fingers brushing dust on a piano, rain tapping the roof—establishes a tactile realism that never wavers. The camera lingers on soil, water, and breath; sound design isolates the hum of life support against the silence of the void. When Kelvin meets the phantom of his dead wife Harey (Natalya Bondarchuk), the scene avoids specter tricks. Instead, Tarkovsky frames her across a table, her face half-lit, her voice flat as she repeats “I don’t remember us.” The actor’s stillness, the slow push-in, the absence of music, all force the viewer to witness grief…
URL: https://hadrien-cornier.github.io/Culture-Calendar/events/solaris.html

15. [9/10] MIRROR
AFS | 2026-08-01 | 15:30
Hook: Tarkovsky's shattered timeline of war, memory, and fractured love becomes a rhythmic visual poem.
Review: ★ Rating: 9/10

🎭 Artistic Merit – Tarkovsky executes a radical structural experiment that abandons linear narrative to dramatize discontinuous shards of memory. The film layers three eras—rural childhood in the 1930s, the Great Patriotic War, and post-war maturity—into a prismatic reflection of the director’s own life . Technical mastery appears in the mesmerizing camera movements and self-consciously painterly compositions that create a cumulative, rhythmic effect rather than a plot-driven sequence . Specific imagery like a field whipped suddenly by wind and a gas lantern flickering out anchors the abstraction in tangible physical reality . The soundtrack integrates Bach’s music and the director’s father, Arseny Tarkovsky, reading his own poetry, which deepens the emotional texture with…
URL: https://hadrien-cornier.github.io/Culture-Calendar/events/mirror.html

16. [9/10] Blade Runner: The Final Cut
Paramount | 2026-08-04 | 19:30
Hook: Ridley Scott’s restored neo-noir masterpiece, Vangelis-scored, dystopian Los Angeles.
Review: ★ Rating: 9/10

🎭 Artistic Merit – Ridley Scott’s Blade Runner: The Final Cut executes a total aesthetic takeover of the science fiction genre through its dense, rain-slicked mise-en-scène and revolutionary retro-fitted futurism. The 2007 restoration corrects the 1982 release’s flaws by removing the intrusive voiceover and the contrived “happy ending,” allowing the film’s visual grammar to dominate without distraction . Doug Trumbull’s visual effects blend seamlessly with Larry Paull’s production design, creating a cityscape where neon billboards reflect off perpetual rain and towering corporate spires emit fire like apocalyptic oil rigs . Rutger Hauer delivers the film’s emotional core in the role of Roy Batty; his final monologue, “I’ve seen things you people wouldn’t believe,” anchors…
URL: https://hadrien-cornier.github.io/Culture-Calendar/events/blade-runner-the-final-cut.html

17. [9/10] Reservoir Dogs
Paramount | 2026-08-06 | 19:30
Hook: Tarantino's heist-gone-wrong pressure cooker, fueled by pop songs and fractured loyalties.
Review: ★ Rating: 9/10

🎭 Artistic Merit 
Tarantino turns a barebones crime setup into a pressure cooker. The warehouse scenes are staged with ruthless economy, and the fractured chronology is not a gimmick, it is the engine that keeps suspicion alive. The ensemble is sharp across the board, especially Harvey Keitel’s shaken professionalism, Tim Roth’s panic, and Steve Buscemi’s nervous, fast-talking self-preservation. The film also knows how to use sound and silence, with everyday pop songs and clipped dialogue giving way to sudden violence that lands harder because the movie has built so much slack into the room.

✨ Originality 
This is a debut that arrived sounding like nobody else. The heist itself is mostly withheld, which was a bold move for a crime film and still feels pointed. Tarantino b…
URL: https://hadrien-cornier.github.io/Culture-Calendar/events/reservoir-dogs.html

18. [9/10] 12 Angry Men
Paramount | 2026-08-08 | 15:30
Hook: A jury room becomes a pressure cooker of bias and relentless argument.
Review: ★ Rating: 9/10

🎭 Artistic Merit, a tightly controlled chamber piece built almost entirely from argument, reaction, and pacing. The film turns a single jury room into a pressure cooker, and the direction keeps finding new angles, new rhythms, and new tensions inside that narrow space. The ensemble work is unusually sharp. Each juror arrives with a distinct posture, voice, and bias, and the performances make those differences legible without overstatement. The craft is disciplined, not flashy, and that restraint is the point.

✨ Originality, high. The premise is simple, but the execution avoids the usual courtroom drama habits. Instead of leaning on spectacle, the film stages moral conflict through dialogue, silence, and group dynamics. It understands that persuasion is procedural and psyc…
URL: https://hadrien-cornier.github.io/Culture-Calendar/events/12-angry-men.html

19. [9/10] Fargo
Paramount | 2026-08-09 | 19:30
Hook: Flat winter landscapes frame violent schemes and a quietly brilliant police chief.
Review: ★ Rating: 9/10

🎭 Artistic Merit – Joel and Ethan Coen’s “Fargo” is built with severe control and dry comic timing. The winter landscapes are not decoration, they are part of the machinery, flattening the characters into a world that looks indifferent to their schemes. Frances McDormand’s Marge Gunderson gives the film its anchor, a police chief who speaks in plain, local phrases and still outthinks everyone around her. The staging is clean, the violence is abrupt, and the cuts often land on jokes that get sharper because they arrive so late.

✨ Originality – The film turns a kidnapping plot into something stranger and more precise, a crime story about greed, routine, incompetence, and self-deception. Its regional speech, its deadpan tone, and its refusal to romanticize criminals give it…
URL: https://hadrien-cornier.github.io/Culture-Calendar/events/fargo.html

20. [9/10] TWO SEASONS, TWO STRANGERS
AFS | 2026-08-10 | 19:00
Hook: A delicate, jagged diptych of rain-drenched oceans and snow-covered villages.
Review: ★ Rating: 9/10

🎭 Artistic Merit
Sho Miyake executes a structurally jagged, emotionally precise bifurcation that refuses to settle into a single mood. The cinematography by Yuta Tsukinaga captures rain-drenched oceans and snow-covered villages with a clarity that turns weather into character . Miyake’s use of mise en abyme—the transition from screenwriter Li’s notebook to the fictional summer encounter between Nagisa and Natsuo—is not a gimmick but a deliberate engine that drives the film’s exploration of subjectivity . The film’s 89-minute runtime feels neither padded nor rushed; it sustains tension through silence and awkward dialogue rather than plot stakes, as seen when Nagisa and Natsuo wade into the ocean exchanging halting words . This is not minimalism for affect; it is a rigorous…
URL: https://hadrien-cornier.github.io/Culture-Calendar/events/two-seasons-two-strangers.html

21. [9/10] GANJA AND HESS
AFS | 2026-08-13 | 20:30
Hook: Bill Gunn's raw, fragmented vampire ritual of blood and desire.
Review: ★ Rating: 9/10

🎭 Artistic Merit
Bill Gunn rejects the polished gloss of mainstream horror to forge a jagged, sensual aesthetic rooted in 16mm texture and dreamlike dissonance. The film’s visual language treats black skin as a bright surface that glistens against the vivid, almost violent crimson of blood, creating a Baroque composition where every shot carries weight . Gunn’s direction is moody and rebellious, embracing fragmentation and non-linear deviation rather than rushing toward narrative clarity . Technical execution feels urgent rather than unpolished; the fractured narrative structure and haptic visuality supply a vital charge that makes the experience unforgettable despite its rough edges . The camera work avoids the "blind" criticism some detractors offer, instead using the sh…
URL: https://hadrien-cornier.github.io/Culture-Calendar/events/ganja-and-hess.html

22. [9/10] THE PIANO
AFS | 2026-08-13 | 17:45
Hook: Silence and hands on keys drive Jane Campion’s stark colonial isolation tale.
Review: ★ Rating: 9/10

🎭 Artistic Merit – Jane Campion executes a film where silence functions as the primary dialogue, forcing the camera to do the work of expression. The saturation of the New Zealand landscape, filmed by Stuart Dryburgh, creates a claustrophobic Gothic atmosphere that mirrors Ada’s internal confinement . Michael Nyman’s music score avoids traditional orchestration, using repetitive, sharp piano motifs that drive the narrative tension rather than merely accompanying it . Holly Hunter’s performance relies on physical rage and precise hand gestures to convey a mute woman’s fury, particularly in the scene where she screams into the piano while her fingers strike the keys . The film’s structure uses circularity, echoing visual motifs of water and mud to trap the viewer in Ada’s st…
URL: https://hadrien-cornier.github.io/Culture-Calendar/events/the-piano.html

23. [9/10] THE PASSION OF JOAN OF ARC with Live Score by The Silent Light
AFS | 2026-08-21 | 19:00
Hook: Silent film's raw faces of suffering
Review: ★ Rating: 9/10

🎭 Artistic Merit – Dreyer achieves formal flawlessness through a ruthless reductionist style that eliminates depth perception, flattening the screen into a medieval relief against plain white backgrounds . The film is comprised mostly of extreme close-ups that refuse to establish clear spatial relationships, a rule-breaking technique that should render the narrative unintelligible but instead forces the viewer to confront the raw micro-expressions of the actors . Renée Falconetti’s performance is the engine of this mastery; her face registers flickers of anguish and resolve in such stark detail that the acting transcends dialogue, conveying meaning entirely through expression . Rudolph Maté’s cinematography captures these nuances with a discipline that turns the courtroom…
URL: https://hadrien-cornier.github.io/Culture-Calendar/events/the-passion-of-joan-of-arc-with-live-score-by-the-silent-light.html

24. [9/10] Blow-Up + Blow Out
Paramount | 2026-08-26 | 19:00
Hook: Two thrillers where evidence becomes a trap, sound and vision betray.
Review: ★ Rating: 9/10

🎭 Artistic Merit 
This paired work, clearly invoking the photographic obsession of Blow-Up and the surveillance panic of Blow Out, earns its place through control of tension, sound, and visual suspicion. The strongest scenes are built around acts of looking and listening, a camera lens, a blown-up image, a tape playback, a scream buried in static. That is where the craft bites hardest, because the films turn evidence into a trap. The editing, especially in the sound-driven investigation material, keeps the viewer inside the same uncertain loop as the protagonist.

✨ Originality 
The basic idea is familiar now, but at the time, and still in execution, it avoids routine thriller mechanics by making uncertainty the subject. Instead of solving a mystery cleanly, it keeps widen…
URL: https://hadrien-cornier.github.io/Culture-Calendar/events/blow-up-+-blow-out.html

25. [9/10] Subculture Lit - Ice
AlienatedMajesty | 2026-09-06 | 19:00
Hook: Kavan's ice-bound nightmare of obsession blurs addiction allegory with psychological horror.
Review: ★ Rating: 9/10

🎭 Artistic Merit: 9 
✨ Originality: 10 
📚 Cultural Significance: 8 
💡 Intellectual Depth: 9

Anna Kavan’s Ice is a cold, crackling nightmare of obsession that refuses to settle into genre, delivering a plotless chase through a world being swallowed by glacial walls. The novel centers on three unnamed figures: a glass-like girl, her captor “the warden,” and a narrator who pursues her with a craving that feels less like love than addiction. Kavan strips away names and landmarks, forcing the reader into a fever dream where reality fractures as the ice advances.

The narrator’s psychology is the book’s true horror. He oscillates between devotion and violent fantasy, at one point imagining snapping the girl’s wrists or watching her die. This isn’t romance; it’s a study of abuse…
URL: https://hadrien-cornier.github.io/Culture-Calendar/events/subculture-lit---ice---2026-09-06-1900.html

26. [9/10] Fellow Travelers
Opera | 2027-02-06 | 19:30
Hook: A queer opera of desperate love and political terror in 1950s Washington.
Review: ★ Rating: [9/10]

🎭 Artistic Merit
The series executes a high-wire act of period detail and emotional restraint, driven by Matt Bomer’s stoic, hypermasculine Hawkins Fuller and Jonathan Bailey’s idealistic Tim Laughlin. Bomer conveys peril through a locked jaw and averted gaze during the McCarthy-era purge scenes, while Bailey breaks that tension with genuine spiritual yearning in the first meeting at an Eisenhower election rally. Ron Nyswaner’s adaptation avoids the melodrama of standard queer tropes by grounding the romance in the specific terror of the 1950s Lavender Scare, where homosexuals were persecuted alongside communists . The cinematography captures the claustrophobia of Washington D.C. offices and the chaotic hedonism of 1970s disco, creating a visual arc that mirrors the char…
URL: https://hadrien-cornier.github.io/Culture-Calendar/events/fellow-travelers---2027-02-06-1930.html

27. [9/10] Fellow Travelers
Opera | 2027-02-07 | 14:00
Hook: A queer opera of desperate love and political terror in 1950s Washington.
Review: ★ Rating: [9/10]

🎭 Artistic Merit
The series executes a high-wire act of period detail and emotional restraint, driven by Matt Bomer’s stoic, hypermasculine Hawkins Fuller and Jonathan Bailey’s idealistic Tim Laughlin. Bomer conveys peril through a locked jaw and averted gaze during the McCarthy-era purge scenes, while Bailey breaks that tension with genuine spiritual yearning in the first meeting at an Eisenhower election rally. Ron Nyswaner’s adaptation avoids the melodrama of standard queer tropes by grounding the romance in the specific terror of the 1950s Lavender Scare, where homosexuals were persecuted alongside communists . The cinematography captures the claustrophobia of Washington D.C. offices and the chaotic hedonism of 1970s disco, creating a visual arc that mirrors the char…
URL: https://hadrien-cornier.github.io/Culture-Calendar/events/fellow-travelers---2027-02-07-1400.html

28. [8/10] WAITING FOR GUFFMAN
Hyperreal | 2026-07-01 | 19:30
Hook: Guest’s improvisational mockumentary finds dignity in small-town delusion.
Review: ★ Rating: 8/10
🎭 Artistic Merit: 8
✨ Originality: 7
📚 Cultural Significance: 8
💡 Intellectual Depth: 7

Waiting for Guffman is a crisp, affectionate satire that mocks small-town delusion without ever punching down. Directed by Christopher Guest and co-written with Guest’s trademark mock-documentary precision , the film follows Corky St. Clair, a flamboyant Broadway aspirant who lands in Blaine, Missouri, to stage a sesquicentennial musical called Red, White and Blaine . The town’s absurd history includes being the “stool capital of America” because a wooden Blaine stool once impressed President Grover Cleveland .

Corky’s cast is a parade of untalented but earnest locals: Eugene Levy’s dentist believes a song-and-dance man lives inside him , while Fred Willard and Catherine O’Hara play a…
URL: https://hadrien-cornier.github.io/Culture-Calendar/events/waiting-for-guffman.html

29. [8/10] PHANTOM OF THE PARADISE
Hyperreal | 2026-07-03 | 19:30
Hook: De Palma’s garish rock opera: a satirical, gory comic-book musical.
Review: ★ Rating: 8/10

🎭 Artistic Merit – Brian De Palma delivers a brash, fast-paced rock opera that functions as a live-action comic book, capturing the garish glitter of the 1970s music scene with intense precision . The technical mastery lies in how the film merges Faust, The Phantom of the Opera, and Edgar Allan Poe without slowing its momentum, using Paul Williams’ score as the active engine for its satire . Specific execution shines in the shower scene, which De Palma cleverly reinterprets from Psycho to fit the disco context, and in the transformation of William Finley’s face after an accident crushes it, turning the songwriter into the masked Phantom . The direction is ebullient yet seething with disdain, balancing the absurdity of Paul Williams playing a sinister, youthful impresario n…
URL: https://hadrien-cornier.github.io/Culture-Calendar/events/phantom-of-the-paradise.html

30. [8/10] JOSIE AND THE PUSSYCATS
Hyperreal | 2026-07-10 | 19:30
Hook: Scrappy punk-rock satire with Alan Cumming’s sneering record exec glee.
Review: ★ Rating: 8/10

🎭 Artistic Merit – The film executes a satirical jitterbug with technical precision, using Robert Elswit’s lensing to heighten the unreality of a world where advertising bleeds into news broadcasts. Director Harry Elfont and Deborah Kaplan let the camera linger on the grotesque excess of product placement, from Puma sneakers to Tide detergent, turning the movie itself into a carrier for the very corruption it critiques. Alan Cumming’s portrayal of Wyatt Frame, the A&R villain who hires the band without hearing a note, delivers a greasy, specific menace that anchors the farce. The script’s comedy relies on sharp timing rather than slapstick, evident when Carson Daly admits in a cameo that he is a "co-conspirator in a plot to brainwash the youth of America," a moment that la…
URL: https://hadrien-cornier.github.io/Culture-Calendar/events/josie-and-the-pussycats.html
